sections.text:
SCHEDULE-Convention No. 124
CHAPTER 216
MEDICAL EXAMINATION OF YOUNG PERSONS Act No.
(UNDERGROUND WORK) 20 of 1973
13 of 1994
Copyright Ministry of Legal Affairs, Government of the Republic of Zambia
The Laws of Zambia
An Act to provide for the implementation in Zambia of the International Labour
Organisation's Convention Number 124 concerning medical examination of
young persons for fitness for employment underground in mines; and to
provide for matters ancillary to or connected with the foregoing.
[23rd March, 1973]

sections.heading: This Act may be cited as the Medical Examination of Young Persons Short title
sections.text: (Underground Work) Act.

sections.heading: In this Act, unless the context otherwise requires- Interpretation
Subject to the provisions of this Act, the Convention shall have the force of law Convention to have
sections.text: in the Republic. the force of law

sections.heading: No employer shall employ or require to work underground in a mine any Initial medical
sections.text:
person under the age of twenty-one years unless, after a thorough medical examination examination
carried out by or under the supervision of an approved physician, such person is certified
by the physician to be in every way physically fit for such employment or work.
Copyright Ministry of Legal Affairs, Government of the Republic of Zambia
The Laws of Zambia
(2) Nothing in this Act shall be construed as authorising the employment of any
person whose employment is prohibited under any other written law.

sections.heading: In addition to the medical examination mentioned in section four, an employer Periodical medical
sections.text:
shall cause every employee to be medically examined by or under the supervision of an examination
approved physician at least once in every twelve months, and no employee who has been,
as a result of such examination, declared by the approved physician to be unfit for
employment or work underground in a mine shall be allowed to continue such in
employment or work.

sections.heading: The Director of the Bureau may at any time, in writing, authorise any person Inspection
sections.text:
(hereinafter referred to as "an inspector") to inspect the registers and certificates of health
of the employees kept and maintained by any employer in pursuance of the provisions of
this Act.
(2) An employer shall, whenever so required by an inspector, produce to him for
inspection, the registers and certificates mentioned in subsection (1).
(3) Whenever an inspector inspects the registers and certificates of health kept and
maintained by an employer, he shall submit a report of his inspection to the Director of the
Bureau, and the Director shall transmit the same together with his comments thereon to
the Minister.

sections.heading: An employer shall in respect of every employee keep and maintain, in such Registers
Any employer who fails to comply with the provisions of this Act shall be guilty of Penalties
sections.text:
an offence and shall on conviction be liable to a fine not exceeding one thousand five
hundred penalty units or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding six months, or to both.
(As amended by Act No. 13 1994)
Copyright Ministry of Legal Affairs, Government of the Republic of Zambia
The Laws of Zambia

sections.heading: The Minister may, by statutory instrument, make regulations for the carrying Regulations
sections.text:
into effect of the provisions of this Act.
(2) In particular and without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing power, such
regulations may-
(a) prescribe the time, place, nature and procedure of the thorough medical
examination to be made under section four;
(b) prescribe the nature of and procedure in connection with the periodical
medical examination to be made under section five;
(c) prescribe the forms to be used for certificates to be issued under this Act;
and
(d) prescribe the form of the register to be kept by an employer under this Act.
Copyright Ministry of Legal Affairs, Government of the Republic of Zambia
The Laws of Zambia
SCHEDULE
(Section 2)
CONVENTION NO. 124
THE MEDICAL EXAMINATION OF YOUNG PERSONS
(UNDERGROUND WORK) CONVENTION, 1965
CONVENTION CONCERNING MEDICAL EXAMINATION OF YOUNG PERSONS
FOR FITNESS FOR EMPLOYMENT UNDERGROUND IN MINES
Article 1

sections.heading: For the purpose of this Convention, the term "mine" means any undertaking, whether public or private, for the
sections.text:
extraction of any substance from under the surface of the earth by means involving the employment of persons
underground.

sections.heading: The provisions of this Convention concerning employment or work underground in mines include employment or
sections.text:
work underground in quarries.
Article 2

sections.heading: A thorough medical examination, and periodic re-examinations at intervals of not more than one year, for fitness
sections.text: for employment shall be required for the employment or work underground in mines of persons under 21 years of age.

sections.heading: Alternative arrangements for medical supervision of young persons aged between 18 and 21 years shall be
sections.text:
permitted where the competent authority is satisfied on medical advice that such arrangements are equivalent to or more
effective than those required under paragraph 1 of this Article and has consulted and reached agreement with the most
representative organisations of employers and workers concerned.
Article 3

sections.heading: The medical examinations provided for in Article 2-
sections.text:
(a) shall be carried out under the responsibility and supervision of a qualified physician approved by the
competent authority; and
(b) shall be certified in an appropriate manner.

sections.heading: An X-ray film of the lungs shall be required on the occasion of the initial medical examination and, when regarded
sections.text: as medically necessary, on the occasion of subsequent re-examinations.

sections.heading: The medical examinations required by this Convention shall not involve the young person, or his parents or
sections.text:
guardians, in any expense.
Article 4
Copyright Ministry of Legal Affairs, Government of the Republic of Zambia
The Laws of Zambia

sections.heading: All necessary measures, including the provision of appropriate penalties, shall be taken by the competent
sections.text: authority to ensure the effective enforcement of the provisions of this Convention.

sections.heading: Each Member which ratifies this Convention undertakes either to maintain an appropriate inspection service for
sections.text:
the purpose of supervising the application of the provisions of the Convention or to satisfy itself that appropriate inspection
is carried out.

sections.heading: National laws or regulations shall define the persons responsible for compliance with the provisions of this
sections.text: Convention.

sections.heading: The employer shall keep, and make available to inspectors, records containing, in respect of persons under 21
sections.text:
years of age who are employed or work underground-
(a) the date of birth, duly certified wherever possible;
(b) an indication of the nature of their occupation; and
(c) a certificate which attests fitness for employment but does not contain medical data.

sections.heading: The employer shall make available to the workers' representatives, at their request, the information mentioned in
sections.text:
paragraph 4 of this Article.
Article 5
The competent authority in each country shall consult the most representative organisations of employers and
workers concerned before determining general policies of implementation and before adopting regulations in pursuance of
the terms of this Convention.
Article 6
The formal ratifications of this Convention shall be communicated to the Director-General of the International Labour
Office for registration.
Article 7

sections.heading: This Convention shall be binding only upon those Members of the International Labour Organisation whose
sections.text: ratifications have been registered with the Director-General.

sections.heading: It shall come into force twelve months after the date on which the ratification of two Members have been
sections.text: registered with the Director-General.

sections.heading: Thereafter, this Convention shall come into force for any Member twelve months after the date on which its
sections.text:
ratification has been registered.
Article 8

sections.heading: A Member which has ratified this Convention may denounce it after the expiration of ten years from the date on
sections.text:
which the Convention first comes into force, by an act communicated to the Director-General of the International Labour
Office for registration. Such denunciation shall not take effect until one year after the date on which it is registered.

sections.heading: Each Member which has ratified this Convention and which does not, within the year following the expiration of
sections.text:
the period of ten years mentioned in the preceding paragraph, exercise the right of denunciation provided for in this Article,
will be bound for another period of ten years and, thereafter, may denounce this Convention at the expiration of each
period of ten years under the terms provided for in this Article.
Article 9
Copyright Ministry of Legal Affairs, Government of the Republic of Zambia
The Laws of Zambia

sections.heading: The Director-General of the International Labour Office shall notify all Members of the International Labour
sections.text:
Organisation of the registration of all ratifications and denunciations communicated to him by the Members of the
Organisation.

sections.heading: When notifying the Members of the Organisation of the registration of the second ratification communicated to
sections.text:
him, the Director-General shall draw the attention of the Members of the Organisation to the date upon which the
Convention will come into force.
Article 10
The Director-General of the International Labour Office shall communicate to the Secretary-General of the United
Nations for registration in accordance with Article 102 of the Charter of the United Nations full particulars of all ratifications
and acts of denunciation registered by him in accordance with the provisions of the preceding Articles.
Article 11
At such times as it may consider necessary the Governing Body of the International Labour Office shall present to
the General Conference a report on the working of this Convention and shall examine the desirability of placing on the
agenda of the Conference the question of its revision in whole or in part.
Article 12

sections.heading: Should the Conference adopt a new Convention revising this Convention in whole or in part, then, unless the new
sections.text:
Convention otherwise provides-
(a) the ratification by a Member of the new revising Convention shall ipso jure involve the immediate denunciation
of this Convention, notwithstanding the provisions of Article 8 above, if and when the new revising Convention
shall have come into force;
(b) as from the date when the new revising Convention comes into force this Convention shall cease to be open to
ratification by the Members.

sections.heading: This Convention shall in any case remain in force in its actual form and content for those Members which have
sections.text:
ratified it but have not ratified the revising Convention.
Article 13
The English and French versions of the text of this Convention are equally authoritative.
The foregoing is the authentic text of the Convention duly adopted by the General Conference of the International
Labour Organisation during its Forty-ninth Session which was held at Geneva and declared closed the twenty-third day of
June, 1965.
